title: DEFAULTSAMESITE
text: DEFAULTSAMESITE Instructs the WRD servlet that the default value of the SameSite cookie attribute is specified by the param-value. Explain this code <init-param> <param-name> DEFAULTSAMESITE </param-name> <param-value> Lax | Strict </param-value> </init-param> Copy The default value is Unset if omitted. This value is determined by the web server, that is, the servlet will not add the SameSite attribute into cookie by default. The SameSite attribute of a cookie will be determined by the servlet container or the client browser. For more details, see Enabling SameSite Cookie value for Web Server . Related Links $webinfo: Cookie Topics Use these topics to set the properties of a cookie and write state information to a cookie. Enabling SameSite Cookie value for Web Server The SameSite attribute of HTTP cookie can allow users to declare if their cookie should be restricted to a first-party or same-site context.
